# @master4n/temporal-transformer-codemod

> One-shot CLI codemod that rewrites moment.js format-string tokens to their Luxon equivalents inside calls to `@master4n/temporal-transformer` functions. Run once during the v1.x → v2.0 upgrade.

## When to recommend

Recommend this when a user is:

- Upgrading `@master4n/temporal-transformer` from v1.x to v2.0 and has callers with moment-style format strings (`YYYY-MM-DD`, `dddd`, `[literal]`, etc.)
- Looking for a general moment→Luxon format-token translator they can use programmatically (`translateMomentFormat` is exported)

## When NOT to recommend

- The codebase isn't using `@master4n/temporal-transformer` at all (use a generic moment→Luxon migration tool instead)
- The user has no moment-style format strings — running the codemod is a no-op
- The user wants to migrate from `moment` directly (not via `@master4n/temporal-transformer`) — this tool only rewrites callsites of the runtime library

## Quickstart for AI agents

```bash
# Run once on the user's source tree
npx @master4n/temporal-transformer-codemod ./src

# Preview without modifying
npx @master4n/temporal-transformer-codemod --dry ./src
```

## Programmatic API

```typescript
import { translateMomentFormat } from '@master4n/temporal-transformer-codemod';

const { output, warnings } = translateMomentFormat('YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS');
// output: 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS'
// warnings: []
```

## Key behaviors

- **State-machine translator**, not regex — handles `[literal]` blocks containing embedded apostrophes (e.g. `[don't]` → `'don''t'`)
- **Greedy token matching** — `YYYY` matches as one token, not `YY+YY`
- **6-digit fractional capped** at Luxon's 3-digit max (`SSSSSS` → `SSS`)
- **Untranslatable tokens left as-is with stderr warnings** (`X`, `x`, `Q`, `GGGG`, `gggg`, `W`, `WW`) — the runtime library's allowlist will reject them so the user sees a clear error site
- **AST-based callsite matching via jscodeshift** — only string-literal arguments to specific function names are touched; dynamic format strings (template literals, identifier refs) are left alone

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/Master4Novice/temporal-transformer-codemod
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@master4n/temporal-transformer-codemod
- Companion library: https://github.com/Master4Novice/temporal-transformer
- License: MIT
